info.title: dotnet
info.description: Command line tool to watch for source file changes during development and restart the dotnet command.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show command line help.

options.name: --diagnostics
options.description: Enable diagnostic output. [default: False]

options.name: --cli-schema
options.description: Display the command schema as JSON. [default: False]

commands.name: add
commands.description: .NET Add Command

arguments.name: PROJECT | FILE
arguments.description: The project file or C# file-based app to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for a project file. [default: <path>]

commands.name: package
commands.description: Add a NuGet package reference to the project.

options.name: --version
options.description: The version of the package to add.

options.name: --framework
options.description: Add the reference only when targeting a specific framework.

options.name: --no-restore
options.description: Add the reference without performing restore preview and compatibility check. [default: False]

options.name: --source
options.description: The NuGet package source to use during the restore.

options.name: --package-directory
options.description: The directory to restore packages to.

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action (for example to complete authentication). [default: False]

options.name: --prerelease
options.description: Allows prerelease packages to be installed. [default: False]

options.name: --project
options.description: The project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.

options.name: --file
options.description: The file-based app to operate on.

arguments.name: packageId
arguments.description: Package reference in the form of a package identifier like 'Newtonsoft.Json' or package identifier and version separated by '@' like 'Newtonsoft.Json@13.0.3'.

commands.name: reference
commands.description: Add a project-to-project reference to the project.

options.name: --framework
options.description: Add the reference only when targeting a specific framework.

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action (for example to complete authentication). [default: False]

options.name: --project
options.description: The project file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one.

arguments.name: PROJECT_PATH

commands.name: build
commands.description: .NET Builder

options.name: --use-current-runtime
options.description: Use current runtime as the target runtime. [default: False]

options.name: --force
options.description:
Force all dependencies to be resolved even if the last restore was successful.
This is equivalent to deleting project.assets.json. [default: False]

options.name: --framework
options.description: The target framework to build for. The target framework must also be specified in the project file.

options.name: --configuration
options.description: The configuration to use for building the project. The default for most projects is 'Debug'.

options.name: --version-suffix
options.description: Set the value of the $(VersionSuffix) property to use when building the project.

options.name: --no-restore
options.description: Do not restore the project before building. [default: False]

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action (for example to complete authentication). [default: False]

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Set the MSBuild verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic].

options.name: --output
options.description: The output directory to place built artifacts in.

options.name: --artifacts-path
options.description: The artifacts path. All output from the project, including build, publish, and pack output, will go in subfolders under the specified path.

options.name: --no-incremental
options.description: Do not use incremental building. [default: False]

options.name: --no-dependencies
options.description: Do not build project-to-project references and only build the specified project. [default: False]

options.name: --no-logo
options.description: Do not display the startup banner or the copyright message. [default: True]

options.name: --self-contained
options.description:
Publish the .NET runtime with your application so the runtime doesn't need to be installed on the target machine.
The default is 'false.' However, when targeting .NET 7 or lower, the default is 'true' if a runtime identifier is specified. [default: False]

options.name: --no-self-contained
options.description: Publish your application as a framework dependent application. A compatible .NET runtime must be installed on the target machine to run your application. [default: False]

options.name: --runtime
options.description: The target runtime to build for.

options.name: --arch
options.description: The target architecture.

options.name: --os
options.description: The target operating system.

options.name: --disable-build-servers
options.description: Force the command to ignore any persistent build servers. [default: False]

options.name: --target
options.description: Build these targets in this project. Use a semicolon or a comma to separate multiple targets, or specify each target separately.

arguments.name: PROJECT | SOLUTION | FILE
arguments.description: The project or solution or C# (file-based program) file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for a project or solution.

commands.name: build-server
commands.description: Interact with servers started from a build.

commands.name: shutdown
commands.description: Shuts down build servers that are started from dotnet. By default, all servers are shut down.

options.name: --msbuild
options.description: Shut down the MSBuild build server. [default: False]

options.name: --vbcscompiler
options.description: Shut down the VB/C# compiler build server. [default: False]

options.name: --razor
options.description: Shut down the Razor build server. [default: False]

options.name: --runtime

options.name: --verbosity

options.description: The directory containing the build artifacts to clean.

options.name: --artifacts-path
options.description: The artifacts path. All output from the project, including build, publish, and pack output, will go in subfolders under the specified path.

options.name: --no-logo
options.description: Do not display the startup banner or the copyright message. [default: True]

options.name: --disable-build-servers
options.description: Force the command to ignore any persistent build servers. [default: False]

commands.name: list
commands.description: List references or packages of a .NET project.

arguments.description: The project or solution file to operate on. If a file is not specified, the command will search the current directory for one. [default: <path>]

commands.description: List all package references of the project or solution.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Set the MSBuild verbosity level. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic].

options.name: --outdated
options.description: Lists packages that have newer versions. Cannot be combined with '--deprecated' or '--vulnerable' options. [default: False]

options.name: --deprecated
options.description: Lists packages that have been deprecated. Cannot be combined with '--vulnerable' or '--outdated' options. [default: False]

options.name: --vulnerable
options.description: Lists packages that have known vulnerabilities. Cannot be combined with '--deprecated' or '--outdated' options. [default: False]

options.name: --framework
options.description: Chooses a framework to show its packages. Use the option multiple times for multiple frameworks.

options.name: --include-transitive
options.description: Lists transitive and top-level packages. [default: False]

options.name: --include-prerelease
options.description: Consider packages with prerelease versions when searching for newer packages. Requires the '--outdated' option. [default: False]

options.name: --highest-patch
options.description: Consider only the packages with a matching major and minor version numbers when searching for newer packages. Requires the '--outdated' option. [default: False]

options.name: --highest-minor
options.description: Consider only the packages with a matching major version number when searching for newer packages. Requires the '--outdated' option. [default: False]

options.name: --config
options.description: The path to the NuGet config file to use. Requires the '--outdated', '--deprecated' or '--vulnerable' option.

options.name: --source
options.description: The NuGet sources to use when searching for newer packages. Requires the '--outdated', '--deprecated' or '--vulnerable' option.

options.name: --interactive
options.description: Allows the command to stop and wait for user input or action (for example to complete authentication). [default: False]

options.name: --format
options.description: Specifies the output format type for the list packages command.

options.name: --output-version
options.description: Specifies the version of machine-readable output. Requires the '--format json' option.

options.name: --no-restore
options.description: Do not restore before running the command. [default: False]

-e VAR1=abc -e VAR2=def -e VAR3=ghi

options.description:
Run tests that match the given expression.
Examples:
Run tests with priority set to 1: --filter "Priority = 1"
Run a test with the specified full name: --filter "FullyQualifiedName=Namespace.ClassName.MethodName"
Run tests that contain the specified name: --filter "FullyQualifiedName~Namespace.Class"
See https://aka.ms/vstest-filtering for more information on filtering support.

See https://aka.ms/vstest-report for more information on logger arguments.

options.name: --output
options.description: The output directory to place built artifacts in.

options.name: --artifacts-path
options.description: The artifacts path. All output from the project, including build, publish, and pack output, will go in subfolders under the specified path.

options.description: Enable verbose logging to the specified file.

options.description: Do not build the project before testing. Implies --no-restore. [default: False]

options.description:
The directory where the test results will be placed.
The specified directory will be created if it does not exist.

options.name: --collect
options.description:
The friendly name of the data collector to use for the test run.
More info here: https://aka.ms/vstest-collect

options.name: --blame
options.description:
Runs the tests in blame mode. This option is helpful in isolating problematic tests that cause the test host to crash or hang, but it does not create a memory dump by default.
When a crash is detected, it creates an sequence file in TestResults/guid/guid_Sequence.xml that captures the order of tests that were run before the crash.
Based on the additional settings, hang dump or crash dump can also be collected.
Example:
Timeout the test run when test takes more than the default timeout of 1 hour, and collect crash dump when the test host exits unexpectedly.
(Crash dumps require additional setup, see below.)
dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-crash
Example:
Timeout the test run when a test takes more than 20 minutes and collect hang dump.
dotnet test --blame-hang-timeout 20min
[default: False]

options.name: --blame-crash
options.description:
Runs the tests in blame mode and collects a crash dump when the test host exits unexpectedly. This option depends on the version of .NET used, the type of error, and the operating system.
For exceptions in managed code, a dump will be automatically collected on .NET 5.0 and later versions. It will generate a dump for testhost or any child process that also ran on .NET 5.0 and crashed. Crashes in native code will not generate a dump. This option works on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Crash dumps in native code, or when targetting .NET Framework, or .NET Core 3.1 and earlier versions, can only be collected on Windows, by using Procdump. A directory that contains procdump.exe and procdump64.exe must be in the PATH or PROCDUMP_PATH environment variable.
The tools can be downloaded here: https://docs.microsoft.com/sysinternals/downloads/procdump
To collect a crash dump from a native application running on .NET 5.0 or later, the usage of Procdump can be forced by setting the VSTEST_DUMP_FORCEPROCDUMP environment variable to 1.
Implies --blame. [default: False]

options.name: --blame-crash-dump-type
options.description: The type of crash dump to be collected. Supported values are full (default) and mini. Implies --blame-crash.

options.name: --blame-crash-collect-always
options.description: Enables collecting crash dump on expected as well as unexpected testhost exit. [default: False]

options.name: --blame-hang
options.description: Run the tests in blame mode and enables collecting hang dump when test exceeds the given timeout. [default: False]

options.name: --blame-hang-dump-type
options.description: The type of crash dump to be collected. The supported values are full (default), mini, and none. When 'none' is used then test host is terminated on timeout, but no dump is collected. Implies --blame-hang.

options.name: --blame-hang-timeout
options.description:
Per-test timeout, after which hang dump is triggered and the testhost process is terminated. Default is 1h.
The timeout value is specified in the following format: 1.5h / 90m / 5400s / 5400000ms. When no unit is used (e.g. 5400000), the value is assumed to be in milliseconds.
When used together with data driven tests, the timeout behavior depends on the test adapter used. For xUnit, NUnit and MSTest 2.2.4+ the timeout is renewed after every test case,
For MSTest before 2.2.4, the timeout is used for all testcases.

options.name: --no-logo
options.description: Run test(s), without displaying Microsoft Testplatform banner [default: True]

options.name: --configuration

commands.name: version
commands.description:
'dotnet workload search version' has three functions depending on its argument:
      1. If no argument is specified, it outputs a list of the latest released workload versions from this feature band. Takes the --take option to specify how many to provide and --format to alter the format.
         Example:
           dotnet workload search version --take 2 --format json
           [{"workloadVersion":"9.0.201"},{"workloadVersion":"9.0.200.1"}]
      2. If a workload version is provided as an argument, it outputs a table of various workloads and their versions for the specified workload version. Takes the --format option to alter the output format.
         Example:
           dotnet workload search version 9.0.201
           Workload manifest ID                               Manifest feature band      Manifest Version
           ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
           microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.current          9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3
           microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net6             9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3
           microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net7             9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3
           microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net8             9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3
           microsoft.net.sdk.android                          9.0.100-rc.1               35.0.0-rc.1.80
           microsoft.net.sdk.ios                              9.0.100-rc.1               17.5.9270-net9-rc1
           microsoft.net.sdk.maccatalyst                      9.0.100-rc.1               17.5.9270-net9-rc1
           microsoft.net.sdk.macos                            9.0.100-rc.1               14.5.9270-net9-rc1
           microsoft.net.sdk.maui                             9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24453.9
           microsoft.net.sdk.tvos                             9.0.100-rc.1               17.5.9270-net9-rc1
           microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.current      9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7
           microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net6         9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7
           microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net7         9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7
           microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net8         9.0.100-rc.1               9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7
      3. If one or more workloads are provided along with their versions (by joining them with the '@' character), it outputs workload versions that match the provided versions. Takes the --take option to specify how many to provide and --format to alter the format.
         Example:
           dotnet workload search version maui@9.0.0-rc.1.24453.9 ios@17.5.9270-net9-rc1
           9.0.201
